26/06/2025, 17:19 Industry contribution levy | AUSTRAC

Australian Government
“ AUSTRAC

Industry contribution levy

Home / Business / Core guidance / Industry contribution levy

The industry contribution levy is an annual payment that some reporting entities must
pay. This levy covers AUSTRAC’s operating costs.

Who pays the levy

Whether you have to pay the levy depends on your earnings and the number and value
of transactions you have reported to us. Usually, only medium to large businesses are
required to pay the levy. These are businesses with one or more of the following:

e earnings of A$100 million or more
e alarge number of transaction reports relative to other entities

e ahigh total value of transaction reports lodged with AUSTRAC during a calendar
year, relative to other entities.

If you receive an invoice from AUSTRAC, you must pay the levy.

How to pay your industry contribution
levy

You can pay your industry contribution by BPAY, credit card, cheque or electronic funds
transfer (EFT). You will find details of these payment methods are on your invoice.

Credit card payment details are provided in the next section.

https://www.austrac.gov.au/business/core-guidance/industry-contribution-levy 1/4

26/06/2025, 17:19 Industry contribution levy | AUSTRAC

Pay your industry contribution levy by
credit card

You can pay your industry contribution levy by credit card through the Reserve Bank of
Australia's Government EasyPay service.

This is a secure service provided to AUSTRAC by the Reserve Bank of Australia using
the Optus Smartpay service

You will need:

e¢ acurrent Visa, MasterCard or American Express card

e your reference number, abbreviated as 'Ref' in the 'Payment options' section of
your invoice.

Pay your invoice with Government EasyPay

Credit card payment fee

A fee applies to transactions made using EasyPay. This fee is not subject to goods and
services tax (GST) and is equal to the fee AUSTRAC incurs.

You will be told the fee amount and transaction total before you confirm your payment.

Government EasyPay processing times

Your payment needs to reach us on or before its due date. Transactions made on a
weekend, national public holiday or after 69m Sydney time will be processed on the next
business day.

Transaction receipt

You will receive a receipt from Government EasyPay for the AUSTRAC industry
contribution payment and the credit card payment fee.

By clicking on this link you will leave the AUSTRAC website and enter the Government
EasyPay service for credit card payments.

https://www.austrac.gov.au/business/core-guidance/industry-contribution-levy 2/4

26/06/2025, 17:19 Industry contribution levy | AUSTRAC

How we calculate the levy

The amount AUSTRAC collects via the levy is based on our annual operating expenses.
Every year we publish the formula we plan to use to calculate the levy for that year.

Although the exact formula changes from year to year, the levy your business is charged
(if any) is based on a combination of:

e your most recent published earnings

e the number and value of transactions you reported to AUSTRAC in the previous
calendar year.

We consult with industry groups and other interested people every year. We welcome
submissions about the proposed formula.

When there is an open consultation about the industry contribution, you will find a link on
this page.

After the consultation is over and the final formula is decided, it is made official by a
Ministerial Determination.

What you need to do

To make sure you are invoiced the correct amount you must:

e keep your business earnings up to date in AUSTRAC Online

e submit transaction threshold reports and international funds transfer
instruction reports when required.

If you believe your invoice is incorrect

If you believe we have invoiced you incorrectly, or for the wrong amount, complete and
submit a levy dispute form (Word, 71KB).

Related pages

Update your annual earnings
